Sunday March 29, 2026 | Mille Fleurs Heritage House, St Clair

Hop, Hunt and Heritage 2

Welcome to the grounds. Explore the history, find the hidden creatures, and enjoy the games we used to play.

The Heritage Quest

Discover the real stories of Mille Fleurs

You are standing inside a piece of Trinidad and Tobago's history. Mille Fleurs has stood on this corner of St Clair since 1904. The Heritage Quest is a guided journey through the grounds and rooms of this house, following the stories of the people who shaped it.

Pick up your Quest Passport at the registration table near the fountain. Follow the clues to our Marshal Stops, get your passport stamped, and uncover parts of this property that most people pass their whole lives without ever entering.

Sign-up closes at 3:30 PM. Free and open to all families.

Interested in hosting your private event here? Tell us below.

The Optometrist Today AR Egg Hunt

Trinidad was once part of a vast Ice Age continent. Today, the animals that roamed that world have returned to Mille Fleurs.

1Search the grounds for hidden Easter eggs.
2Open an egg to find a QR code and a secret code word on the slip inside.
3Enter the code word below to unlock that animal.
4Tap the button, point your phone at the grounds, and watch what appears. Leave the egg exactly where you found it.
Found in Trinidad

Harlan's Ground Sloth

Paramylodon harlani

The size of a car and built like a tank. Its bones were pulled from Trinidad's own asphalt deposits, the same tar that still lies beneath this island today. Point your phone at the open lawn to see it moving.

Found in Trinidad

Shasta Ground Sloth

Nothrotheriops shastensis

Smaller than Harlan's but unmistakable. Two different ground sloth species walked Trinidad at the same time, grazing a savanna that once stretched all the way to Venezuela.

Found in Trinidad

Teratorn

Teratornis merriami

A flying predator with a wingspan stretching up to four metres. Its fossils have been confirmed in Trinidad. Point your phone straight up at the sky above the property to watch it soar.

From Trinidad's world

American Mastodon

Mammut americanum

Elephant-like creatures called gomphotheres walked Trinidad, confirmed from our asphalt deposits. The Mastodon was their close relative, roaming the same vast Ice Age continent this island was once part of.

From Trinidad's world

Saber Toothed Cat

Smilodon fatalis

The most iconic predator of the Ice Age. While its bones have not yet been found in Trinidad's tar seeps, this apex hunter ruled the ancient Americas that this island was once part of. Point your phone at the shadows.

From Trinidad's world

Columbian Mammoth

Mammuthus columbi

Thirteen feet tall with tusks longer than a car. The Columbian Mammoth roamed the Ice Age Americas, the same world Trinidad was part of before the sea rose and made us an island some 11,000 years ago.

You Found All Six!

You are an official Prehistoric Explorer of Mille Fleurs.

Take a photo of your animals on the lawn and tag @NationalTrustTT

3D models provided for educational use by the Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County.

What's On

Activities across the grounds this afternoon

🎊

Maypole Dancing

Watch the ribbons weave or join in. Running throughout the afternoon at the bmobile Games Yard.

Front yard, right side
🎮

Giant Yard Games

Giant Snakes and Ladders, Connect 4, Jenga, Tic Tac Toe and more. Walk up and play throughout the afternoon.

bmobile Games Yard
🥁

Bobolee Beating

One of Trinidad and Tobago's most spirited Easter traditions. Find the Bobolee on the grounds and take your turn.

Front yard
🧢

Easter Bonnet Competition

Three categories: Biggest and Boldest, Most Creative, Best Local Touch. Bring your bonnet from home. Register at the Games Yard table.

Registration closes 3:30 PM
🎏

Kite Making with a Local Master

Build your own traditional kite from scratch. Sign up at the Camboulay Room door when you arrive. Spots fill quickly.

Limited spots
🎏

Chinese Embassy Kite Workshop

Two sessions during the afternoon. Families build traditional Chinese kites together in the Upstairs Ballroom.

Upstairs Ballroom
📖

Children's Storytelling

Two sessions in the Prada Room. No sign-up needed. Local authors reading work rooted in Trinidad and Tobago life and childhood.

Prada Room
🐴

Horse Riding

Ponies at the back of the property near the market entrance, courtesy of the TTPS Mounted Branch.

Back of property
🎨

Face Painting

Find the face painter near the vendors at the back of the house. First come, first served.

Near the Mews
🍲

Food and Refreshments

Vendors at the covered market at the back of the house. Corn soup, hot cross buns, snow cone, popcorn and more.

The Mews

Find Your Way

Key locations across the property

  • A
    Front Verandah
    Bonnet Competition parade and judging
  • B
    Front Yard, right side
    bmobile Games Yard, Maypole Dancing, Quest and Bonnet registration
  • C
    Near the fountain
    Heritage Quest sign-up
  • D
    Camboulay Room door
    Local Kite Making sign-up
  • E
    Upstairs Ballroom
    Chinese Embassy Kite Workshop
  • F
    Salvatori Room
    Traditional Games and Heritage Quest stop
  • G
    Prada Room
    Children's Storytelling
  • H
    Left side of the grounds
    Picnic lawn, Dairy Dairy activation
  • I
    The Mews, back of house
    Food vendors, face painting, horse riding
  • J
    Restrooms
    At the back of the house near the market area

The National Trust of Trinidad and Tobago

We safeguard the built and natural heritage of Trinidad and Tobago. By becoming a member, you help protect spaces like Mille Fleurs for the next generation.

Become a Member

You have already seen what this space can do

The verandah. The fountain. The ballroom. Leave your details and we will call you.

Perfect. We will be in touch shortly.

Weddings and ceremonies

The verandah, the fountain, the yard. A setting that needs no decoration to be extraordinary.

Corporate and private events

Dinners, launches, receptions, gatherings. Spaces that carry history into every occasion.

Cultural and community events

NTT actively partners with organisations whose work connects to heritage, culture and community life.

Photography and film

The architecture, the light, the grounds. Available by arrangement for shoots and productions.
